title: Alpha-1-Proteinase Inhibitor (Human): MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

What should I do if I forget a dose? 
If you miss a dose or an appointment to receive alpha-1-proteinase inhibitor, contact your doctor right away.
What side effects can this medication cause? 
Alpha-1-proteinase inhibitor may cause side effects. Tell your doctor if any of these symptoms are severe or do not go away: 
headache runny nose, coughing diarrhea nausea tiredness muscle soreness bruising or bleeding at the site of injection Some side effects can be serious. If you experience any of these symptoms, call your doctor immediately or get emergency medical treatment: 
hives, swelling in the mouth or throat, itching, chest tightness, trouble 
breathing, wheezing, fainting or dizziness fever, chills, tiredness, and other signs of infection Alpha-1-proteinase inhibitor may cause other side effects. Call your doctor if you have any unusual problems while taking this medication.
If you experience a serious side effect, you or your doctor may send a report to the Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) MedWatch Adverse Event Reporting program online ( https://www.fda.gov/Safety/MedWatch ) or by phone ( 1-800-332-1088 ).
